title: Add support for containers.conf
body:
vendor in c/common config pkg for containers.conf

Signed-off-by: Qi Wang <qiwan@redhat.com>
Signed-off-by: Daniel J Walsh <dwalsh@redhat.com>

```

Fixes:https://github.com/containers/libpod/issues/4084
@rhatdan looks like gating is complaining of an incomplete man page change.  Should this still be a WIP?
@TomSweeneyRedHat Just removed the [WIP].  No idea what is the problem with the man page.  Ran it locally and it passed.  Just rebased and repushed.  Will see what happens.

The manpage checks fail because podman errors:
```
[+0000s] checking: podman 
[+0000s] panic: runtime error: invalid memory address or nil pointer dereference
[+0000s] [signal SIGSEGV: segmentation violation code=0x1 addr=0x220 pc=0x1662021]
```
`./bin/podman --help` triggers it locally as well

Just one more bug to get this merged, but I am at a loss to what is causing this. Of course it does not happen locally.
@edsantiago Any ideas?
Looks like a flake. I restarted to check.
Well it is a very consistent flake.  Seems to be happening all the time.
Looking into it. Did you try as remote?

I can't even test it on my machine. The remote-client doesn't even work properly on master. I see control characters when running a container and build is failing trying to copy data over the varlink bridge.

```
[+0127s] # error running container: error configuring network list if1 for [/bin/sh -c apk add nginx]: failed to create bridge "do-not-use-google-subnets": could not add "do-not-use-google-subnets": numerical result out of range
```
`contrib/cirrus/99-do-not-use-google-subnets.conf` is copied into `/etc/cni/net.d` for CI. My first guess, and it's just a wild guess, is that something has changed in containernetworking-plugins. I'll look into it further this morning as time allows, but wanted to throw this out in case it makes sense to anyone.
Well I have attempted to get this to happen on my local box and have tried to manually trigger it but nothing. If we could get a local reproducer, this would be a lot easier to debug.
Fixes: https://github.com/containers/libpod/issues/3587
@rhatdan needs a rebase
Crap. I didn't see that there's a `common/pkg/caps` ... now we have `libpod/pkg/capabilities` and that.
I'll merge the two.
https://github.com/containers/common/pull/65

@TomSweeneyRedHat @mheon @baude @jwhonce @vrothberg @giuseppe PTAL
Finally passes tests.  Not sure what is wrong with rdoproject.org.
Please don't merge today, before I cut 1.8.2 final. Will do a review this afternoon.

/hold
@mheon is in charge of cancelling, unless I get sent back to the drawing board

/hold cancel

Full review delayed until tomorrow
@mheon @vrothberg @giuseppe @TomSweeneyRedHat @QiWang19 @baude Could some give this a review, so we can finally get it merged...

@mheon @vrothberg @QiWang19 @TomSweeneyRedHat @baude @jwhonce PTAL
Fixed up issues.
LGTM

@mheon @vrothberg @QiWang19 @TomSweeneyRedHat @baude @jwhonce @giuseppe PTAL
Could we get this in...
/lgtm

@baude @jwhonce @mheon @rhatdan ... while working on pull I found that the v2 binary is now failing for all invocations with `Error: eventer creation: No support for journald logging`. Looks like this PR introduced it.

I will look into it but we _really_ need some sort of CI for podmanV2.
K. It turns out we just need to specify the `systemd` build tag.
